sutta: on the book of the force or chapter of the force now the house father anatha pindika came to visit the exalted 1 as he said at 1 side the exhaust the exalted 1 said this to the house father anata pindika [Music] house father there are these 4 kinds of bliss to be won by the householder who enjoys the pleasures of sense from time to time and when occasion offers what for the bliss of ownership the bliss of wealth the bliss of deathlessness the bliss of blamelessness and what house father is the bliss of ownership in this case a clansmen has wealth acquired by energetic striving amassed by strength of arm 1 by sweat lawful and lawfully gotten at the thought wealth is mine acquired by energetic striving amassed by strength of arm 1 by sweat lawful and lawfully gotten bliss comes to him satisfaction comes to him this house father is called the bliss of ownership and what is the bliss of wealth in this case house father a clansmen by means of wealth acquired by energetic striving amassed by strength of arm won by sweat lawful and lawfully gotten both enjoys his wealth and thus meritorious deeds therewith at the thought by means of world at quiet i both enjoy my wealth and do meritorious deeds bliss comes to him satisfaction comes to him this house father is called the bliss of wealth and what is the bliss of deathlessness in this case a clansman owes no debt great or small to anyone at the thought i owe no that great or small to anyone bliss comes to him satisfaction comes to him this house father is called the bliss of deathlessness and what is the bliss of blamelessness in this case house father the aryan disciple is blessed with blameless action of body blameless action of speech blameless action of mind at the thought i am blessed with blameless action of body speech and mind bliss comes to him satisfaction comes to him this is called the bliss of blamelessness such house father are the 4 kinds of bliss to be won by the householder who enjoys the pleasures of sense from time to time when occasion office that's the end of the suta
commentary: [Music] you see in this utah the buddha said that lay people can enjoy these few things the first 2 is the bliss of ownership and the bliss of wealth they are related firstly the first 1 the bliss of ownership is because a person because of energetic striving by his own sweater he has lawfully gotten all this [Applause] [Music] things that that he owns so because of that now he gets a lot of satisfaction from it and the second 1 wealth is about the same he also sweats for it acquires a lot of wealth and with his wealth he does meritorious deeds that's a wise man because whatever we enjoy in the world we are digging into our uh bank our bank account our bank account of blessings so as we enjoy life we are using up our blessings so it's always very important to plant back the seeds of meritorious deeds so that another day they will sprout and you will enjoy you can enjoy the fruits of your meritorious deeds again and that's why uh the buddha always advises and then when you have wealth to do meritorious deeds so that you can get to enjoy it again and again so from here from from the first 2 items here you can see very clearly that buddha never condemned the acquiring of property and wealth only thing that the buddha condemn is unwholesome actions uh if we uh do unwholesome actions to acquire property or wealth or use the property or wealth and to do unwholesome actions then the buddha would condem such action but if we know how to use our wealth wisely and then it is good to have to have wealth also and then the third 1 is the bliss of deathlessness you don't owe anybody anything and you get satisfaction from knowing that you don't owe people anything the last 1 is very important the bliss of blamelessness if we don't have blame worthy action now we have not done something that is blameable through the 3 karmas of body speech and mind then when we are about to pass away especially the mind is happy it does not have a lot of remorse that's why you find people who keep sila keep their perception when they're about to pass away their mind is not disturbed there's no remorse in their mind so because of that their mind is very clear very clear until the moment they pass away and their mind is very clear whereas if you see some other person who doesn't keep the preception the mind the remorse disturbs him because of remorse and the thought of having done wrong the mind becomes very restless and disturbed and these people they become sinal you see that some people see now even before they pass away and they are really chattering nonsense and words and when they are about to pass away and they see fearful sights in them because they are about to be reborn into a woeful plane and all the things that consciousness conjures up in their mind are all frightening things whereas another person like recently i have seen an old man in ganga about 81 years old just about 3 weeks away ago he passed away and went to see him he had a lot of visions but there were good visions pleasant visions and he was very happy and he died very peacefully [Music] so coming back to this uh wealth uh the chances of committing and wholesome actions uh increase now when we have more wealth uh it's um difficult to find somebody with a lot of wealth and power who does not misuse the wealth and power so the only thing that can help us now walk the proper path is the dhamma that's why it's very important to have the dhamma as our refuge we always take refuge in the buddha dharma and sangha so we have the dharma as our guide it's nothing more important than an understanding of life from the dharma so because the dharma teaches us to conduct ourselves well even you have wealth also you can enjoy and still you won't make a mess of life and you will have a good rebirth even if you have a lot of wealth and enjoy this life and you go to the next life and still you enjoy because of the guidance you get from the dharma now we come to another suta
